behaviors.description: Generated function signature matches the interface specified in the natural-language description.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP applies: correctness depends on the model interpreting the description and producing the right parameter names/types/return type. A stubbed model would hide signature-understanding failures. Rejected higher level Workflow: behavior does not require multiple agentic decisions in sequence; it is one generation quality check.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real LLM on a curated set of interface-constrained prompts; parse generated code signatures and assert exact match to expected interface contract.

behaviors.description: Orchestration runs attempts in order and exits early on first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS applies at module boundaries (orchestrator, model client wrapper, test runner, attempt state). This is control-flow wiring, not model-quality dependent. Integration-brake checks: no real OS/network/subprocess required; no dependence on LLM output quality; no multi-agentic chain required.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator with deterministic stubs: attempt1 fails, attempt2 passes, attempt3 should not run. Assert call order, stop condition, and returned winning attempt.

behaviors.description: When first generation usually fails, the live iterative loop can still succeed within allowed attempts.
behaviors.justification: Requires multiple real agentic steps in sequence (generate -> test -> refine -> regenerate -> retest), so MULTI-STEP applies. Also LLM-DEP applies because success depends on model adaptation quality across attempts. Rejected lower Agentic: single-call agentic tests cannot catch cross-attempt loop failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end with real LLM on a function known to fail first attempt frequently; assert at least one subsequent attempt passes before attempt limit and loop state transitions are correct.

behaviors.description: On test failure, refinement prompt builder includes failed test names and failure messages in the next prompt.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at local handoff boundary (test result payload -> prompt builder -> next request payload). No real model judgment needed to verify inclusion. Integration-brake: no OS/network/subprocess requirement, no AI-output quality dependency, no multi-agentic sequence needed for this assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Simulate failed test results through real internal modules and assert the built next-prompt payload contains each failed test name and associated failure message.

behaviors.description: Next live attempt changes implementation in response to prior failure context instead of resubmitting identical code.
behaviors.justification: Needs at least two real model decisions in sequence with context carryover (initial generation + refinement generation), so MULTI-STEP applies. A stub would trivially pass/fail without validating model responsiveness to failure context. Rejected Agentic-only: single-step model checks cannot verify iterative response behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute two-attempt live loop where first attempt fails; assert second generated implementation is materially different and aligned with injected failure context.

behaviors.description: Service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns structured error containing all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic orchestration/bookkeeping across modules (attempt manager, test result collector, error serializer). No real LLM-quality dependency is required; failing outputs can be stubbed. Integration-brake checks pass: no mandatory OS/network/subprocess and no multi-agentic correctness requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator with deterministic always-fail stubs for three attempts; assert termination at exactly 3 and error payload schema/content includes per-attempt test results.

behaviors.description: Full non-agentic pipeline wiring (generate call, test execution, bookkeeping) works end to end with deterministic stubbed model outputs.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly constrains to non-agentic deterministic stubs and checks module wiring. This is exactly integration scope. Rejected higher levels: System disqualified because no real OS/network/subprocess requirement is stated; Agentic/Workflow disqualified by 'non-agentic' and stubbed outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Run complete in-process pipeline with fixed stub outputs and deterministic test outcomes; assert generation invocation, test execution, and attempt bookkeeping are correctly connected end-to-end.

behaviors.description: Full live loop (generate, test, refine, retest) solves at least one known hard function that fails on first attempt.
behaviors.justification: Explicitly requires multi-step live agent pipeline with real model behavior across attempts, so MULTI-STEP + LLM-DEP both apply. Mocking would hide adaptation failures and falsely pass. Rejected lower Agentic: single-call or single-decision tests cannot validate the full iterative pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run real end-to-end iterative loop on at least one benchmark function selected because first attempt fails; assert eventual pass within attempt cap and verify attempt sequence integrity.
